content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission
August L4, !99O
Art Bloomer
City Counci Imember
city of rrvine
4 Pintail
rrvine, cA 927 f4
Dear Mr. Bloomer:
You are seeking advice as a member of the city of Irvine city
Council regarding your duties and responsibilities under the
conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Political Reforn Act (the
rrAct" ) .1
The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of July 20, L99o and in prior conversations.
OUESTION
A decision currently before the city council concerns a
development agreement for construction of a high-rise office
building and monorail. You are a principal officer of an
industrial security company that presently provides security guard
services at the site where the construction, if approved by the
city council, would occur. May you participate in the development
agreement decis j-on?
CONCLUSTON
You may participate in the decision on the development agree-
ment provided the decision will not have a foreseeable material
f i,nancial impact on the industrial security company for which you
serve as a principal. officer.
FACTS
McDonnell Douglas Realty company (,'McDonnell Douglasrr) cur-
rently has a development agreement pending before the city council
1 co.r".rr."nt Code Sections 810OO-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Comnission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. A11 references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O3O? . (916)322.5660
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-484


File No. A-90-4 84
Paqe 2
to construct a high-rise office building and a monoraiL on its
property (rtDouglas Pl-azarr) .
You are a senior vice-president of an industriaL security
company, American Protective Services, fnc. (rrAPsr') Iilhich cur-
renlly has a contract to provide security guard services at
Douglas Plaza. APS, headquartered in oakland, California, has
11,ooo employees and annual sales of approxinately $18o rnillion.
You are one of nine shareholders of APS; your ownership
interest consists of shares of restricted corn:non stock srhich
constitute less than L/4 of 1 percent of the entire company. As
the fifth principal officer of APS, you receive a salary as well
as a share in the company's profits and dividends; twice yearly
bonuses calculated to overall company performance are also
provided.
The current contract betveen APs and McDonnell Douglas
provides APS srith annual revenues of approxinately $135,000. You
have indicated that if the developrnent agreement is approved, ad-
ditional revenues to APS from I'IcDonnell Douglas Bay result because
of a potential need for additional security guard services-
ANALYSIS
The Act prohibits a public official 
from making, participat-
ing in, or using his official position to influence a governmental
deiision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a finan-
cial interest. 
(section 87100.) A public official 
has a finan-
cial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a rnaterial financial effect, disti,nguish-
able from i.ts effect on the public aenerally, on the official, 
a
member of official's 
imrnediate family, or on
(a) Any busi.ness entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect i,nvestnent worth
one thousand dolLars ($1,000) or nore.
(c) Any source of income, ... aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars (S250) or more ... lrithin 12
months prior to the tirne when the decision is rnade.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
offi.cial is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of nanagement.
Section 87103 (a) , (c) , (d) .
Because you are an elected member of the City of Irvine city
council, you are a public official 
(Section 82048) and must there-
fore disqualify yourself fron any city council decision which will
have a reasonably foreseeable naterial financial effect,
distinguishable frorn the effect on the public Arenerally, on you,


FiIe No. A-9 o-4 8 4
Page 3
on any business entity in which you have a requisite investment
interest, on any source of incone to you of $25o or more, or on
any business entity for which you serve as an officer or director.
(section 87103. )
You have an investment interest in APs, presumably worth more
than $1,ooo. In addition, APS is a source of income to you, pre-
surnably of $250 or more, and you are an officer in APs. Accord-
ingIy, you must disqualify yourself from any decision that will
have a ieasonably foreseeable and material financial effect on APS
which is distinguishable from. the effect on the public generally.
Foreseeable ttaterial FiDaacial Effect
The effect of a decision is reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will occur. While certainty
-is not required, an effect that is merely a possibility is not
reasonably foreseeable. (Downev cares v. Downey communitv
Development com. (1987) 196 Cal.App.3d 983; fn re Thorner (1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 198, coPY enclosed.)
You have indicated in your letter that (1) APs currently
provides security guard services to McDonnell Douqlas at Douglas
blaz^, (2) the matter pending before the city council concerns the
deveJ.opment of additional construction at Douglas PLaza, (3) as a
result of the construction a potential need for additional
security guard coverage at Douglas Plaza could arise, and (4) APS
could realize additional revenues from the provision of such ad-
ditional coverage. on the basis of these facts, it appears that a
financial irnpact on APS as a result of the pending decision before
the city council is reasonably foreseeable.
In order to determine whether the foreseeable effect of a
decision is materiaL, the conmission has adopted Regulation 18702
et seq. Whether the effect of the decision is positive or
negative is of no consequence under the Act. 
(Younq Advice Let-
tei, uo. A-89-149, copy encJ,osed.) on the basis of the facts you
have provided, it appears that APS is a business entity whose
involvement in the pending decision is indirect, rather than
direct. 
As such, Regulation La702.2 is applicable (copy
enclosed).
Thj-s regulation provides that the effect of a decision is
materi.al- when certain financial impacts result on a business
entity that is indirectly involved in a decision.
The inpact on APS resulting from the pending Douglas Plaza
decision will be considered rnaterial, thereby requiring your
abstention frorn participating in the decision, if the appropriate
financiaL threshold is attained. The financial thresholds for the


File No. A-9 0-484
Page 4
impact vary, depending on the size of the business entity; the
larger the business entity, the higher the thresholds.z
You have indicated that APS employs approximately 11,000
individuals and has approximately $180 nillion in annual sa1es.
Because this infornation al-one is insufficient for us to determine
the appropriate subsection of the regulation that is to be ap-
plied, we are unable to provide you with advice under Regulation
18702.2. Instead, we leave to you the deterraination as to the
appropriate subsection applicable to APS so as to perurit the de-
termination whether the Douglas Plaza decision will iropact APS in
a manner that $rould be considered naterial.
Public GeDerally
Even if the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a de-
cision is naterial, disqualification is required only if the ef-
fect is distinguishable from the effect on the public generally.
(Section 87103.) For the city, the Public consists of all
residents of the city. 
Thus, disqualification is required unless
the decisi,on will affect APS in substantially the same manner as
it will affect all, or a significant segment, of the residents of
the city. 
(Regulation 18703, copy enclosed.) Based on the
circumstances you have described, it is apparent that the inpact
of the Douglas P1aza decision on APS will not be the same as the
effect on the public or a significant segTnent of the public. 
Most
of the public, apparently, will- not be inpacted by the decisj-on at
all,3
fn your letter you have indicated that security services are,
or have been, provided by APS to other entities in the city and
2 Business entities listed on the Nev, York or American stock
Exchanges are covered by 18702.2(a). Entities listed on the Na-
tional Association of Securities Dealers National Market List
(NASDNML) are covered by 18702 (b) . Entities listed on the Paci,f ic
stock Exchange and listed on the california DePartment of corpora-
tions' Eligible Securities List are covered by 1-8702.2(c) . Enti-
ties not listed on the New York or American Stock Exchanges but
meeting the financial standards for listing on the most recent
Fortune Magaz.ine Directory of the 500 fargest U.S. industrial and
non-industrial corporations are covered by 18702.2(d). Entities
which meet the financial standards for Iisting on the New York
stock Exchange but are not listed on the New York or American
Stock Exchanges nor the NASDNML are covered by 18702.2(e). Enti-
ties which neet the financial standards for listing on the NASDNML
but are not listed on the Ne$, York or American Stock Exchanges nor
the NAsDNlrtL are covered by 18702.2(tr. Entities which are not
covered by L87o2.2(a), (b), (c), (d), (e), or (f) are covered by
L87 02 .2 (g) .
?- Copies of fn re Owen (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 77 and In re Legan
(r.985) 9 FPPC ops, 1, Conmission opinions which explain the appli-
catj-on of the concept of public generalLy, are enclosed for your
information.


File No. A-9o-484
Page 5
that it is tikely such entities wilL appear before the city
council in the future. 
The advice in this letter, however, is
linited to the pending decision before the city council concerning
Douglas P1aza. Because questions concerning these other entities
are at this point hypothetical and specul,ative, $re are unable to
provide advice concerning these other entities. 
(RegRrlation fa329,
copy enclosed.) While our advice in this letter concerns only the
pending decision involving Douglas P1aza, you are encouraged to
evaluate future siroilar situation in a manner as described in this
letter .
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this roat-
ter, please contact ne at (916) 322-59oL.
sincere Iy,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
A. 1," ,).--'
fl[,+ "-- ' l\ k" .
Byi Jonathan S.'Rothman
f 
counsel, Legal Division
\

Enc Iosures
